This work is taking place on land adjacent to Bures Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A third party has arranged for trees near the overhead electricity lines to be trimmed and has asked us to temporarily switch off the power so this can be carried out safely. Some of these trees have grown close to the cables and could cause damage, particularly during poor weather. Allowing this work to take place helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps the network safe and reliable. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut trees away from 2 spans of overhead cables. This work will help reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced planned and unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Queen Street.

Our reference number for this work is INCD-109337-C. I hope that the power coming on earlier is of benefit to you. We always aim to provide a 10/10 service, if you feel we have not delivered you a 10/10 service then please call us on 105.

Good afternoon, this is a message from Donna at UK Power Networks regarding the planned power cut which is currently in progress today the 16th June 2026 in the RH13 postcode area.
The power was switched off in stages to enable our contractors to replace and upgrade electricity poles. They also carried out works to replace and upgrade some overhead electricity cables with improved insulated cables on Two Mile Ash Road.
Our contractors have advised us that customers due to be restored at 16:30 will be restored early by approximately 15:15 as works have progressed better than anticipated.
Once completed and all power has been restored to the area this will improve the reliability of our electricity network and will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut.
To provide some additional support, we had our customer support vehicle on site. Hopefully you were able to visit Jack, and he was able to help you during the planned power cut.
Our reference number for this work is INCD-17780-W.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A third party has arranged for trees near the overhead electricity lines to be trimmed and has asked us to temporarily switch off the power so this can be carried out safely. Some of these trees have grown close to the cables and could cause damage, particularly during poor weather. Allowing this work to take place helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps the network safe and reliable. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. This work will help reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts in the future. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, A longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. This work is taking place near Peterhouse Crescent.

Sent by Adam at 14:49 - We can now confirm that all power in the Rectory Lane area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected overhead network fault, which our engineers removed a fallen tree to re-erect the grounded overhead cabling.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 12:06 - I'm texting to say I am glad to report that power to the Walderslade Road area should now be back on.

I'm sorry for the continued disruption recently. We believe there may be a fault on our underground electricity cabling in the area, but currently it has not developed to a stage that our staff can pinpoint it. Their tests have proven inconclusive, so for now they have replaced our safety equipment at the substation to get your power back on as quickly as possible.

This may happen again before we're able to fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about this, or if your power isn't on call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
I appreciate your continued patience this morning. We want to be as transparent as possible as we know having multiple power cuts is disruptive and worrying, so I would like to help explain why we can't always fix a problem like this right away.

Underground electricity cabling is insulated for safety and if a fault develops, these materials can re-seal due to heat passing through them when our safety equipment operates and your power is lost. This covers any potential fault again, meaning our staff cannot locate it using their tests or specialist equipment.

We'll know more once our staff contact us again, so I'll be in touch again shortly.

Sent by Kevin at 11:45.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Walderslade Road area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 23:19 - Hello again. Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut, I know the lights being off is a worry.

Our staff have advised that they have run into further complications with the ongoing repairs to the faulty underground electrical equipment. Due to the delay this has caused, they've advised a new time of 00:00-01:00 to have your power back on.

I know another delay is frustrating; our engineers always try to give us the best estimate that they can but due to the safety elements of working with live electricity, sometimes they have to adjust their repairs at short notice.

I'm sorry if this has caused you to change any plans you've made, but we'll be here to update you until the lights are safely back on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by Decasta at 14:42

Sent by Decasta at 17:19 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 18:28 - We can now confirm that all power in the South Street CM23 area should be back on.

To fix the issue permanently we're trying to find the underlying root cause, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the South Street CM23 area.

As any issues that currently exist on our network have affected our equipment at the substation, engineers will start their testing there. They will then check our network equipment throughout the area to try and identify any problems, before trying to restore power.

Our estimated time frame of 21:00-22:00 hasn't changed, but if this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. This can involve ground excavations, traffic management equipment or specialist team. As soon as our staff can safely update us, we'll give you the latest news. Sent by Simon at 18:17.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 18:05 - We're sorry your power has gone off again. We are sending our engineers back to the South Street CM23 area to investigate why.

Currently, our time frame for power to be restored is between 21:00 and 22:00, but once our staff arrive on site and assess the situation, this time may change. As there have been other power cuts recently it's difficult to know what repairs will be needed to restore your power safely, so time frames we provide throughout have to be estimated.

The results of our engineer's tests on our network will let us know if our estimate needs to be adjusted, so we'll update you as soon as they have arrived on site and investigated.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 22:15 - Hi David here  from the messaging team. We can now confirm that all power in the St. Johns Avenue MK42 area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi, this is David - I wanted to let you know I have just taken over for the night shift, so I'll be here until tomorrow morning making sure you have the latest news from our staff.

I know that power being off may be especially worrying for you as we head later into the evening, but our field teams work 24 hours so I'll let know further news as soon as it comes in.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
I appreciate your continued patience this evening. I know having power cuts as often as this must be very disruptive, and our staff are doing everything they can to get this resolved.

They are still testing our network in the area, and depending on the local infrastructure this can take some time. However, once they update us we'll know if our estimated time frame needs to change and why, and I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

In the meantime, if we can offer any support or advice, our team are always available to talk on 105.

Sent by Simon at 21:51.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the St. Johns Avenue MK42 area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by David at 22:37
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Just to let you know our staff have now arrived back on site in the Portman Park area. They are going to test our equipment thoroughly to check if the underlying issue can be located before they attempt to restore your power again.

I know having power cuts this often must be causing you a lot of problems, and our staff are doing everything they can to keep the power on. Our time frame remains unchanged, and we're keeping in contact with our staff regularly to make sure you have the best information to plan ahead.

I'll update you as soon as we hear more from them. Sent by David at 22:16.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 22:50 - Thanks for your patience this evening.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our underground cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Parkside area to investigate the power cut affecting approximately 270 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 22:34 - Our engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is faulty underground electrical equipment affecting approximately 270 properties.

Specialist engineers from our control room are also working to divert power remotely, so it's possible your power could come back on at any point. We can't always restore everyone this way. That's why we're also sending staff to the area to check our equipment.

Every fault is different, so our time frames have to be estimated but currently we hope to have power back on between 00:30-01:30. We must stress that it may become necessary for this to change, depending on what our engineers find.

We'll keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
